  "text": "The Grafana configuration loading system is implemented primarily in `pkg/setting/setting.go`, `pkg/setting/expanders.go`, `pkg/setting/provider.go`, and `pkg/api/admin.go`.\n\n## INI File Parser & Configuration Loading Chain\n\n`pkg/setting/setting.go` is the core file. The loading sequence is:\n\n1. `NewCfgFromArgs()` → `Load()` → `loadConfiguration()` → `parseINIFile()`\n\n`loadConfiguration()` (line 963) orchestrates the full override chain:\n1. Loads `conf/defaults.ini` as the base configuration using the `gopkg.in/ini.v1` library\n2. Calls `applyCommandLineDefaultProperties()` to apply `cfg:default.*.*=value` CLI args\n3. Calls `loadSpecifiedConfigFile()` to merge the user-specified config (e.g., `custom.ini`) on top of defaults\n4. Calls `applyEnvVariableOverrides()` to apply `GF_<SECTION>_<KEY>=value` environment variables\n5. Calls `applyCommandLineProperties()` to apply `cfg:<section>.<key>=value` CLI args (highest priority)\n6. Calls `expandConfig()` from `pkg/setting/expanders.go` to resolve `${VAR}` and `${__file:path}` references\n7. Calls `parseINIFile()` to map INI values to `Cfg` struct fields\n\n## Environment Variable Override Logic\n\nIn `pkg/setting/setting.go`:\n- `EnvKey(sectionName, keyName)` (line 855) converts a section+key to the `GF_<SECTION>_<KEY>` format\n- `applyEnvVariableOverrides()` (line 744) iterates all sections/keys, calls `EnvKey()`, and applies matching env vars\n- `DynamicSection` / `SectionWithEnvOverrides()` (lines 1568, 1605) provide runtime env var override for code reading section keys directly after initial config load\n\n## Default Values\n\n`conf/defaults.ini` is the canonical source of default values for all configuration sections. `parseINIFile()` in `pkg/setting/setting.go` (line 1165) also hardcodes defaults for many settings using `MustString`, `MustBool`, `MustInt`, etc. Individual section readers in `pkg/setting/setting_*.go` files (e.g., `setting_smtp.go`, `setting_data_proxy.go`, `setting_unified_alerting.go`, etc.) implement per-section default logic.\n\n## Sensitive Config Masking for /api/admin/settings\n\n`RedactedValue(key, value string)` (line 660 in `pkg/setting/setting.go`) masks values whose key matches patterns like PASSWORD, SECRET, PRIVATE_KEY, TOKEN, CLIENT_SECRET, ENTERPRISE_LICENSE, etc. It also calls `RedactedURL()` to redact passwords embedded in connection URLs.\n\n`pkg/setting/provider.go`: `OSSImpl.Current()` iterates all INI sections and calls `RedactedValue()` on every value before returning the `SettingsBag` to the API layer.\n\n`pkg/api/admin.go`: `AdminGetSettings()` (line 29) calls `hs.SettingsProvider.Current()` (which returns the already-redacted bag) and then `getAuthorizedSettings()` further filters keys by RBAC permissions (`ActionSettingsRead`) before returning the JSON response at `GET /api/admin/settings`."
}

Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## Configuration File Parsing and Override Chain in Grafana

### Core Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `pkg/setting/setting.go` | Main config loading, INI parsing, env var overrides, sensitive value masking |
| `pkg/setting/expanders.go` | Config value expansion (`${VAR}`, `${__file:path}`) |
| `pkg/setting/provider.go` | Settings provider interface; `OSSImpl.Current()` applies redaction for the API |
| `pkg/api/admin.go` | `GET /api/admin/settings` handler with RBAC filtering |
| `conf/defaults.ini` | Default values for all configuration sections |
| `pkg/setting/setting_*.go` | Per-section readers (smtp, data_proxy, unified_alerting, plugins, rbac, etc.) |

### Override Chain (in order of increasing priority)

1. `conf/defaults.ini` — base defaults loaded by `loadConfiguration()`
2. `applyCommandLineDefaultProperties()` — `cfg:default.*.*=val` CLI args
3. `loadSpecifiedConfigFile()` — merges user's `custom.ini`
4. `applyEnvVariableOverrides()` — `GF_<SECTION>_<KEY>=val` environment variables
5. `applyCommandLineProperties()` — `cfg:<section>.<key>=val` CLI args (highest priority)
6. `expandConfig()` — resolves `${VAR}` and `${__file:path}` interpolations

### Sensitive Value Masking

`RedactedValue()` in `setting.go` masks keys matching patterns like `PASSWORD`, `SECRET`, `TOKEN`, `CLIENT_SECRET`, etc., and `RedactedURL()` strips passwords from connection URLs. `OSSImpl.Current()` in `provider.go` applies this to every setting before serving the `/api/admin/settings` response.

  "text": "The Grafana configuration loading system is implemented primarily in `pkg/setting/setting.go`, `pkg/setting/expanders.go`, `pkg/setting/provider.go`, and `pkg/api/admin.go`.\n\n## INI File Parser & Configuration Loading Chain\n\n`pkg/setting/setting.go` is the core file. The loading sequence is:\n\n1. `NewCfgFromArgs()` → `Load()` → `loadConfiguration()` → `parseINIFile()`\n\n`loadConfiguration()` (line 963) orchestrates the full override chain:\n1. Loads `conf/defaults.ini` as the base configuration using the `gopkg.in/ini.v1` library\n2. Calls `applyCommandLineDefaultProperties()` to apply `cfg:default.*.*=value` CLI args\n3. Calls `loadSpecifiedConfigFile()` to merge the user-specified config (e.g., `custom.ini`) on top of defaults\n4. Calls `applyEnvVariableOverrides()` to apply `GF_<SECTION>_<KEY>=value` environment variables\n5. Calls `applyCommandLineProperties()` to apply `cfg:<section>.<key>=value` CLI args (highest priority)\n6. Calls `expandConfig()` from `pkg/setting/expanders.go` to resolve `${VAR}` and `${__file:path}` references\n7. Calls `parseINIFile()` to map INI values to `Cfg` struct fields\n\n## Environment Variable Override Logic\n\nIn `pkg/setting/setting.go`:\n- `EnvKey(sectionName, keyName)` (line 855) converts a section+key to the `GF_<SECTION>_<KEY>` format\n- `applyEnvVariableOverrides()` (line 744) iterates all sections/keys, calls `EnvKey()`, and applies matching env vars\n- `DynamicSection` / `SectionWithEnvOverrides()` (lines 1568, 1605) provide runtime env var override for code reading section keys directly after initial config load\n\n## Default Values\n\n`conf/defaults.ini` is the canonical source of default values for all configuration sections. `parseINIFile()` in `pkg/setting/setting.go` (line 1165) also hardcodes defaults for many settings using `MustString`, `MustBool`, `MustInt`, etc. Individual section readers in `pkg/setting/setting_*.go` files (e.g., `setting_smtp.go`, `setting_data_proxy.go`, `setting_unified_alerting.go`, etc.) implement per-section default logic.\n\n## Sensitive Config Masking for /api/admin/settings\n\n`RedactedValue(key, value string)` (line 660 in `pkg/setting/setting.go`) masks values whose key matches patterns like PASSWORD, SECRET, PRIVATE_KEY, TOKEN, CLIENT_SECRET, ENTERPRISE_LICENSE, etc. It also calls `RedactedURL()` to redact passwords embedded in connection URLs.\n\n`pkg/setting/provider.go`: `OSSImpl.Current()` iterates all INI sections and calls `RedactedValue()` on every value before returning the `SettingsBag` to the API layer.\n\n`pkg/api/admin.go`: `AdminGetSettings()` (line 29) calls `hs.SettingsProvider.Current()` (which returns the already-redacted bag) and then `getAuthorizedSettings()` further filters keys by RBAC permissions (`ActionSettingsRead`) before returning the JSON response at `GET /api/admin/settings`."
}
